channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Japan game maker Nintendo reports lower profit as demand for Switch consoles wanes
item.description: Nintendo’s profits tumbled as sales of its Switch console lost momentum, prompting the Japanese video-game maker to lower its full-year forecasts.
item.dc:creator: Yuri Kageyama, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Cisco researchers highlight emerging threats to AI models
item.description: Cisco security researchers this week detailed a number of threats they are seeing from bad actors trying to infect or attack AI’s most common component – the large language model.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Help! We're drowning in email spam, it's about to get worse and there's nothing we can do to stop it
item.description: Email spam is spiralling out of control, with over 90% of emails classified as such in 2024, report claims.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Linux Foundation’s L3AF brings zero-downtime updates to eBPF network management
item.description: A challenge for enterprises that operate large-scale network infrastructure has long been how to maintain performance while updating critical systems.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Warren presses Treasury secretary over Musk access to payments system
item.description: Sen. Elizabeth Warren (D-Mass.) sent a letter to Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent on Monday demanding to know what access Elon Musk was given to the federal payments system and what safeguards were implemented to protect Americans’ private data and prevent abuses.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, The Hill

item.title: VPN usage skyrockets in DR Congo amid TikTok and X shutdown
item.description: Proton VPN recorded a 7000% increase in sign-ups above baseline as citizens are looking for ways to evade restrictions. Here's all we know so far.
item.dc:creator: chiara.castro@futurenet.com (Chiara Castro), TechRadar

item.title: Big rigs deliver cargo with no humans at the wheel
item.description: First driverless semi delivers 100 loads in Texas, transforming logistics with AI. Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson gives his takeaways.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The Clicks iPhone keyboard is as powerful as it is frustrating
item.description: A funny thing happened after I stopped using Clicks, the keyboard case that effectively turns an iPhone into an oversized Blackberry: The phone by itself suddenly seemed punier. I mean that in terms of both size and mightiness.
item.dc:creator: Jared Newman, Fast Company

item.title: Energy-saving scam uses Elon Musk’s name – Here’s the truth
item.description: A new scam using Elon Musk's name is making the rounds, promoting "energy-saving" devices and falsely linking the Tesla and SpaceX leader to the products.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Abbott bars Texas officials from using DeepSeek, RedNote on government devices
item.description: Texas Gov. Greg Abbott (R) banned state officials from using Chinese-owned technology such as DeepSeek and RedNote on government devices Friday.
item.dc:creator: Sophia Vento, The Hill

item.title: Apple's iOS vulnerability exposes iPhones to stealthy hacker attacks
item.description: Hackers sometimes find ways to exploit iPhones, and Apple recently discovered a vulnerability in iOS that may have been exploited for over a year.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Family of OpenAI whistleblower Suchir Balaji files lawsuit seeking San Francisco police records
item.description: Balaji garnered national attention in October when he went public with accusations that his former employer, OpenAI, was flouting federal copyright laws with its blockbuster chatbot, ChatGPT.
item.dc:creator: Jakob Rodgers, The Mercury News

item.title: Texas governor orders ban on DeepSeek, RedNote for government devices
item.description: AUSTIN, Texas (AP) — Texas Republican Gov. Greg Abbott issued a ban on Chinese artificial intelligence company DeepSeek for government-issued devices, becoming the first state to restrict the popular chatbot in such a manner.
item.dc:creator: NADIA LATHAN, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Apple reports dip in iPhone sales over the holidays, despite AI rollout
item.description: Apple on Thursday disclosed its iPhone sales dipped slightly during the holiday-season quarter, signaling a sluggish start to the trendsetting company’s effort to catch up to the rest of Big Tech in the race to bring artificial intelligence to the masses.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Italy blocked AI app DeepSeek in Apple and Google stores. Here’s why
item.description: The Chinese artificial intelligence app DeepSeek could not be accessed on Wednesday in Apple and Google app stores in Italy, the day after the country’s data protection authority requested information on its use of personal data.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: Sam Bankman-Fried’s parents appealing to Trump to pardon their beloved son: report
item.description: The mother of Sam Bankman-Fried has apparently had a major change of heart about Trump after once leading a top Silicon Valley fundraising network for anti-Trump Democrats.
item.dc:creator: Martha Ross, The Mercury News

item.title: Capital One’s new AI agent will help you buy your next car
item.description: Capital One has launched an AI agent designed to help consumers with one of the most frustrating, time-consuming processes in life: buying a car. The banking giant’s Chat Concierge provides information, makes decisions, and takes action using multiple AI agents.
item.dc:creator: Jocelyn Mintz, Fast Company

item.title: Italy blocks DeepSeek due to unclear data protection
item.description: Italy’s data protection authority Garante has chosen to block the app for the much-hyped Chinese AI model DeepSeek in the country.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Opinion: AI is harming our children. California must step up
item.description: Will lawmakers again permit another addictive technology open to children and stand by as another generation gets hurt?
item.dc:creator: Robert C. Fellmeth, The Mercury News

item.title: 9 Google Chrome features you really should be using
item.description: If you’re like about 70% of computer users worldwide, you use Google’s Chrome browser as your gateway to the web, from conducting research and catching up on news to emailing and interacting with cloud apps. There are several tools built into Chrome that you might not know about, but should.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: US cybersecurity agency's future role in elections remains murky under the Trump administration
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The nation’s cybersecurity agency has played a critical role in helping states shore up the defenses of their voting systems, but its election mission appears uncertain amid sustained criticism from Republicans and key figures in the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTINA A. CASSIDY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Timeline of HPE’s $14 billion bid for Juniper
item.description: AI is a key factor in one of the tech industry’s most significant M&A developments: HPE’s proposed acquisition of Juniper Networks. The $14 billion deal would shake up the industry, and regulatory watchdogs in the U.S. and worldwide have raised antitrust concerns.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: How federal land could generate lots of electricity
item.description: "We estimate 10 to 20 million homes could be served from the electricity that are produced from that renewable energy," said Trieu Mai. The post How federal land could generate lots of electricity first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Tom Temin, Federal News Network

item.title: Amazon sues state agency to block release of company records to Bezos-owned Washington Post
item.description: Amazon is suing a Washington state agency to prevent the release of some company materials to The Washington Post, the newspaper which is owned by Amazon founder Jeff Bezos.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: AI investments face integration, compliance, and skills challenges: survey
item.description: Network engineers report that they see the current and future value in using artificial intelligence for network management and cybersecurity purposes, according to new survey results, but they also anticipate more barriers to successful adoption than their C-suite counterparts.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Cisco touts ‘Internet of Agents’ for secure AI agent collaboration
item.description: When AI agents begin to proliferate, a new, open structure will be needed so they can securely communicate and collaborate together to solve complex problems, suggests Cisco.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Industry Exchange Cyber 2025: Zimperium’s Kern Smith on why securing mobile devices doesn’t necessarily mean locking them down
item.description: With the right continuous monitoring of mobile activity and apps, agencies can avoid restrictive device policies, advises Zimperium’s solutions engineering VP.
item.dc:creator: Tom Temin, Federal News Network

item.title: Microsoft first raises doubts about DeepSeek and then adds it to its cloud
item.description: Despite initiating a probe into Chinese AI startup DeepSeek, Microsoft has added the startup’s latest reasoning model R1, to its model catalog on Azure AI Foundry and GitHub.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Upstart Chinese AI company DeepSeek's founder started out as a low-key hedge fund entrepreneur
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — The 40-year-old founder of China’s DeepSeek, an AI startup that has startled markets with its capacity to compete with industry leaders like OpenAI, kept a low profile as he built up a hedge fund and then refined its quantitative models to branch into artificial intelligence.
item.dc:creator: ELAINE KURTENBACH, Associated Press, NewsNation
